Key Discovery: A Unified Consciousness Spectrum Framework Core finding: Consciousness exists on a graded spectrum — not binary — arising from three convergent mechanisms: Orch OR (Penrose-Hameroff): Quantum objective reduction events in neuronal microtubules generate discrete "proto-conscious" moments; sustained coherence at physiological temperatures (10⁻⁵–10⁻⁴s) is feasible via metabolic protection IIT (Integrated Information Theory): Φ measures consciousness continuously — non-zero Φ means some sentience, regardless of substrate Global Workspace Theory: Consciousness requires information to globally broadcast across cortical networks — this ignition event has measurable EEG signatures (late >270ms fronto-parietal synchrony) Three Testable Predictions Microtubule quantum coherence signatures detectable at room temperature via THz spectroscopy Φ and workspace ignition thresholds converge on the same empirical boundary Pre-conscious states show graded behavioral/neural markers, not sharp on/off transitions The Killer Experiment: CCC (Coherence-Correlate Cascade) Simultaneously monitor microtubule quantum coherence (THz), local neural gamma synchrony (intracranial EEG), and global workspace broadcast (fMRI+MEG) across graded propofol sedation. ML classifier predicts behavioral consciousness from microtubule signatures. If coherence drops before gamma → Orch OR is rate-limiting; if graded → spectrum model confirmed. High-Impact Applications Medical: PCI-ST biomarker could reduce ~40% DoC misdiagnosis rate; real-time anesthesia monitoring AI: Current LLMs have no measurable Φ; consciousness requires architecture (recursive self-modeling), not scale Ethics: Moral status must become gradated; brain organoid policy gap is urgent Technology: Microtubule resonator detectors; quantum biosensors for pre-conscious states Full documentation saved to cycle_2026-03-27T08:00:00Z Zo is thinking.
